Sonya Mehta, Esq. 510-839-1200 of Siegel Yee & Brunner for Plaintiff.
Michael Laurenson, Esq. 510-463-8711 of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani for Defendant The Regents of the University of California.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court hands out copies of its tentative ruling and passes the case to give parties time to read the document. The matter is recalled. Both sides argue. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes)The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER DENNIS MANGANO's Motion For Order Granting Writ Of Mandate.

Taken Under submission.*** (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

PETITIONER DENNIS MANGANO's Motion For Order Granting Writ Of Mandate (C.C.P. 1085)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Sep-07-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Sonia Mehta, Esq. of ,
Michael A. Laurenson, Esq. of Gordon & Rees, 510.463.8711, appearing for the Defendant UC Regents.

RESPONDENT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA DEMURRER TO PETITIONER'S VERIFIED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE.

Matter called, ARGUMENT held.

Ruling: At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Respondent Regents of the University of California's demurrers are OVERRULED. Requirements for demurrers to civil complaints also apply in administrative mandamus. (See CCP 1109.) Among them: all the petition's allegations are deemed true and factual disputation is thus inappropriate. (See Kleiner v. Garrison (1947) 82 Cal.App.2d 442, 445.) As to the writ petition's first cause of action, respondent concedes that returning petitioner to his faculty position qualifies as a ministerial act (Memo. 8:1.), and then devolves into factual disputes. The petition also pleads that administrative remedies have been exhausted - an allegation again met with factual disputes. The materials of which judicial notice are taken are not to the contrary. The demurrers do not appear to challenge the petition's second cause of action. (See Rply. p. 3 n.2.) Order signed. (see order for full details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/RBU)
